Hey everyone, welcome back. This video is going to explain the closer framework. So this is kind of a thousand foot overview of how the selling process is structured and how we've been able to glean away and make this as straight a line as possible in the conversation to keep them on the path of discovery that they should work with you. Okay, so, closer is a, I can't remember the acronym I think, but basically each one of the letters stands for one part of the selling process. So C is clarify why they came in. That is the first thing you're doing with the lead is, hey, what made you opt in? What made you come in today? What made you take the time out of your schedule to prioritize this? What's the main problem you're trying to solve? And so you need to clarify why they came in. And there's going to be even little objections that happen here where people are like, well, I just want to get more information. I just want to check it out. Like, no, but I mean, I don't sign up for free toasters because I already have a toaster. So what's the main reason you came in? Right? What problem are we trying to solve? And then you can dig in a little bit deeper so that they present a problem. Well, I've, you know, kind of fell off. I'm trying to get back in shape. Okay, so you're trying to lose weight or you're trying to gain muscle and then you start clarifying and dig it down. Once you've dug down enough to have a clear goal, clear outcome of what they're trying to achieve, you go to L, which is you're going to label them with a problem. And so you're basically going to summarize and restate the main reason why they came in. Okay, so the main reason you're here today and that you signed up is because you've been struggling in the past with this and you've gained, you know, 50 pounds in the last few years and you're trying to lose weight. Does that sound about right? Yes. Okay, cool. So you won't be upset with me if I help you lose the weight, you know, in a short period of time. You'll still be okay with me. You won't be upset. Ha, ha, ha. Right. And they're like, yes, of course not. Okay. So we go from C to L to now. Oh, so like, okay, so you're trying to lose this weight. Give me some examples. Like what have you done up to this point to get here? Right. What programs? What have you tried to do that you've been unsuccessful with? All right. And then they're going to name all of the things that they've done. We're going to take them through what we call a pain cycle, but it's essentially increasing their awareness of why it's important to them and how many failures they've had to endure to get to the point where they're at. So it's like, they're essentially showing you how much money they've spent, how long they've been doing this, how hard they have tried. And so you want to be thinking about while you're going through the pain cycle is total time cost, total emotional cost, and total different types of things that they've tried. And we'll show you in the pain cycle section how to actually categorize the pains so that you can use them in your pitch to solidify what you're going to be telling them. And one of the key questions to ask here is why do you think you've been unable to achieve this on your own? And then that'll very much flip the conversation to, well, I won't dig too much into this, but that's over in the past pain and we'll get into the pain cycle during the sales training. That's just selling the vacation, which is where you're going to go through the three-pillar pitch, which is fitness, nutrition, accountability, and you can explain it very, very quickly. It's a high gloss over. And the reason for that is the more details you provide someone about the actual nitty-gritty of the program, the more things they have to say no to. And so you really want it to be a high level gloss over and only use a specific detail to overcome a specific obstacle, not to delineate every single thing that's going to be in the program because what happens is when you do that, they won. Have way more things to say no to, but two, it reminds them of how much work is about to happen. And it's a lot easier to get someone to work once they've given you their money than before they've given you their money, especially when it's something like self-improvement, like fitness. So we clarify whether they're, we label them with a problem now that we know what it is. We overview the past pains of why they failed to achieve that in the past. And then after that, we say, you know what, I understand what you've done. I think you might like what we have. Can I tell you about it? And then you'll sell them the vacation fitness, nutrition, accountability. After you sell the vacation, you're going to try and go for the close. And at that point, you will either have closed the sale or you'll explain away concerns, which is the kind of obstacle overcomes. So that's where it's like, well, after I talked to my husband, I have to think about it. I don't make split decisions. I don't have that kind of money, blah, blah, blah, blah. All of those things, all those options are overcomes is explaining away their main concerns. And you have to see it that way is that you're helping them make a decision and you're helping them get out of their own way and really explaining away their fears so that they have the confidence and that you can, you can transfer your confidence and conviction to them so that they can make the right decision for themselves that we both know they should make. I mean, it's why they're there. They want you to help them make the decision that they're having difficulty making for themselves. And then ours reinforce the sale. So even after you close the sale and you've overcome any concerns they have, et cetera, you need to reinforce it. So you need to wow them and really blow them away and make them feel like they really made the right decision. Because the first thing that happens if you don't do this is people get buyer's remorse, especially in an emotional sale like fitnesses. Fitness is an emotional sale. It's a vanity sale. And so people get home and they're like, what did I do? I'm not going to go to the gym, blah, blah, blah, blah. And so you need to reinforce that sale. Make sure they understand that they're in good hands. Make sure that they know that you're going to fulfill your promises, go above and beyond. And we have specific strategies to do that so you can decrease people who get cold feet. And then ultimately strengthen the sale, strengthen their convictions so that they get better results in the long run. So this is the closer framework. You can use it with your teams. You can use it for yourself. You can write down a piece of paper, whatever you want. But these are the pieces, the blocks that have to happen in the sale for you to be successful. Just remember each of these things is you're clarifying whether they're labeling with the problem, labeling them with the problems. And now you can solve it, overviewing what they've done in the past that they have failed to achieve the success they want. And then bridging the gap, using all the things they failed and explaining why our vacation is going to solve all those problems, which it will. And I mean that genuinely, you have to have that conviction. And then help them make the decision. And that's it and reinforce the sale. Okay. That is the closer framework. And let's get into it.
